name: PluginBase
summary: The PluginBase class in an abstract base class for model plug-ins. Model plug-ins have to be inherited from this class. Drawing plug-ins have to be inherited from the DrawingPluginBase. A plug-in is always executed in the plug-in's local coordinate system. The origin of the plug-in's coordinate system is defined based on the first input object or point. In case the first input is an object, the origin of the plug-in's coordinate system is the first input point of the object. In case the first input is a point, the origin of the plug-in's coordinate system is the input point. The X- and Y-axes of the coordinate system are defined in the current plane.

methods.name: DefineInput
methods.summary: The method Tekla Structures calls for the plug-in to query the input. The plug-in must then return a list of input definition instances. The plug-in will be dependent on the items it returns. Dependent means that if any of these items change, for example the user moves the points, the plug-in will be re-run with new input. DefineInput is not called during the re-run, and thus all the actual implementation should be in the Run() method. The maximum number of InputDefinitions in the List is 10.

methods.name: IsDefaultValue(Double)
methods.summary: Returns true if the given value is set to the default value for this type.

methods.name: IsDefaultValue(Int32)
methods.summary: Returns true if the given value is set to the default value for this type.

methods.name: IsDefaultValue(String)
methods.summary: Returns true if the given value is set to the default value (empty string).

methods.name: Run
methods.summary: The main method of the plug-in. It is called after the input has been defined with DefineInput(). This is the "main" method of the plug-in and should contain all the actual implementation.

properties.name: Identifier
properties.summary: The identifier of the executable plug-in instance.
